title: taraxacum caninum
intro: Taraxacum caninum, also known as dog's daisy or lesser dandelion, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family, Asteraceae. The medicinal properties of Taraxacum caninum lie in its leaves, flowers, and roots, which contain flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system, as well as its ability to act as a diuretic and a laxative. Taraxacum caninum is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in capsule or powder form to harness its medicinal properties.
